name: Minecraft: NeoForge
author: ops@refx.example
description: Minecraft NeoForge mod-loader server. The community-driven Forge fork; the default loader for most modern (1.20.2+) modpacks.

installScript.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl jq
cd /mnt/server
MC_VER="${MINECRAFT_VERSION}"
LOADER="${LOADER_VERSION}"
# NeoForge versions are derived from the Minecraft version: e.g. MC 1.21.1 ->
# 21.1.x. When 'latest'/'recommended', resolve the newest matching build from
# the NeoForged maven metadata.
if [ "$LOADER" = "latest" ] || [ "$LOADER" = "recommended" ]; then
  META=$(curl -fsSL https://maven.neoforged.net/releases/net/neoforged/neoforge/maven-metadata.xml)
  # Map e.g. 1.21.1 -> prefix 21.1.  (drop the leading '1.')
  PREFIX=$(echo "$MC_VER" | sed -E 's/^1\.([0-9]+)(\.([0-9]+))?$/\1.\3/; s/\.$/.0/')
  # TODO(impl): the prefix mapping above assumes 1.x.y Minecraft versioning;
  # confirm against the NeoForged maven metadata before relying on it for new
  # snapshots / non-standard version strings.
  LOADER=$(echo "$META" | grep -oE '<version>[^<]+</version>' | sed -E 's/<\/?version>//g' | grep -E "^${PREFIX}\." | grep -v -- '-beta' | tail -n 1 || true)
  if [ -z "$LOADER" ]; then
    LOADER=$(echo "$META" | grep -oE '<version>[^<]+</version>' | sed -E 's/<\/?version>//g' | grep -E "^${PREFIX}\." | tail -n 1 || true)
  fi
  if [ -z "$LOADER" ]; then echo "No NeoForge build found for Minecraft ${MC_VER} (prefix ${PREFIX})" >&2; exit 1; fi
fi
echo "Installing NeoForge ${LOADER} (Minecraft ${MC_VER})"
curl -fsSL -o neoforge-installer.jar "https://maven.neoforged.net/releases/net/neoforged/neoforge/${LOADER}/neoforge-${LOADER}-installer.jar"
java -jar neoforge-installer.jar --installServer
rm -f neoforge-installer.jar neoforge-installer.jar.log
# NeoForge generates run.sh + user_jvm_args.txt +
# libraries/net/neoforged/neoforge/<ver>/unix_args.txt; the startup command
# launches via those arg files (no runnable server jar).
if [ ! -f user_jvm_args.txt ]; then
  echo '# Xmx/Xms are supplied on the command line by the panel' > user_jvm_args.txt
fi
# Normalize the generated arg file to a stable root path so the startup command
# never needs the resolved build number (works with 'latest'/'recommended').
ARGS=$(find libraries -type f -name unix_args.txt | head -n1)
if [ -z "$ARGS" ]; then echo 'unix_args.txt not found after install' >&2; exit 1; fi
cp "$ARGS" unix_args.txt
if [ "${EULA}" = "true" ]; then echo 'eula=true' > eula.txt; fi
echo 'Install complete.'

variables.description: Unused for NeoForge (the server launches via generated arg files); kept for compatibility.
The Minecraft version to install NeoForge for (e.g. 1.21.1).
The NeoForge build version (e.g. 21.1.66). Use 'latest' to auto-resolve the newest matching build from NeoForged maven.
Maximum Java heap size in megabytes (-Xmx).
You must accept the Mojang EULA to run a server.
